headline: Savour the good to settle the system
subtext: Lingering on small good moments helps rebalance an over-alert brain.
steps: Notice a small good moment as it happens.
Stay with it for twenty seconds, letting it register.
Let your body feel that, right now, all is well.
caption:
A nervous system stuck on high alert scans constantly for threat. One way to rebalance it is to deliberately practise the opposite: noticing safety and pleasure.

Savouring means catching a small good moment, a warm drink, sunlight, a laugh, and staying with it for twenty seconds so it truly registers. Done often, it gently tips the nervous system away from threat and towards calm.

For chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, these small deposits of safety add up, and they are a skill you can build.

More practices at https://neurowaze.health

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Deliberately savouring positive moments helps counter the brain's negativity bias and supports nervous-system regulation. Find guided practices in the Neurowaze Resources area: https://neurowaze.health
